texto: I tell people a lot of times, if you want to be a part of something, you never know, you kind of just have to be around. A lot of people don't really have the patience for it, and they don't stick around. Dre and I are still working together, and we have plenty of music for the future.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Anderson Paak sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I tell people a lot of times, if you want to be a part of something, you never know, you kind of …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a trabalho, music, future, útil em momentos de trabalho e future.

frase_original: I tell people a lot of times, if you want to be a part of something, you never know, you kind of just have to be around. A lot of people don't really have the patience for it, and they don't stick around. Dre and I are still working together, and we have plenty of music for the future.

texto: Not everything is going to be handed to you just because you're talented with a big smile. Sometimes you just gotta get out and shoot jumpers for hours and hours and hours. That's something I didn't really get a grasp on until way later, waking up early and treating it like a job if you're serious about it. Get the freak up and, you know, work.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anderson Paak: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Not everything is going to be handed to you just because you're talented with a big smile. Someti…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, work, job pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e job.

That's something I didn't really get a grasp on until way later, waking up early and treating it like a job if you're serious about it. Get the freak up and, you know, work.

texto: My story as an artist has been about trial and error. It's been about artist development, character building, struggle, happiness and failure, family, and music.
